name: Read-only Service-level Cutover Plan
goal: Define a separately approved read-only service-level soak/cutover path for routing Prism GET read surface through Go while preserving Python as rollback and owner for all writes/files/migrations.

allowed: reverse proxy protected by external auth
not_allowed: unprotected Caddy or Flask/Go public endpoint
exposure_boundary.auth_note: Prism still has no built-in API token, Bearer token, or user auth layer.

python_service.name: prism.service
python_service.role: primary runtime, write/file/maintenance owner, rollback target

go_sidecar_service.name: prism-go-readonly.service
go_sidecar_service.role: read-only sidecar for validated GET surface only

routing_owner.phase19_5_plan: Python-side PRISM_GO_READ_ROUTING switch remains the planned first control point
routing_owner.future_caddy_option: Caddy may be considered only after a successful Python-switch soak and separate approval

preflight_required_before_execution: Confirm current Python prism.service is active and healthy
Confirm /api/test, /api/system/migration-status, /api/notes, and one note detail are healthy on Python
Confirm backup exists and can be read
Build or deploy Go sidecar artifact for the target host
Confirm Go sidecar starts with explicit DB path and reports /healthz schema_version >= 16 and sqlite_query_only=true
Confirm Go sidecar binds localhost only
Confirm no POST / PUT / DELETE / PATCH routes are served by Go
Confirm no Caddy public exposure change is included in the first soak

execution_stages.name: No-routing sidecar smoke
execution_stages.description: Start Go sidecar while Python remains the only routed service. Smoke healthz and read endpoints directly on localhost sidecar.
execution_stages.rollback: Stop Go sidecar; Python service remains untouched.

execution_stages.name: Python opt-in read routing soak
execution_stages.description: Enable PRISM_GO_READ_ROUTING=1 and PRISM_GO_READ_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5002 on Python service only after explicit approval. Keep all non-whitelisted routes on Python.
execution_stages.rollback: Unset PRISM_GO_READ_ROUTING or set it to 0, restart Python service, verify routed reads no longer include X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing.

execution_stages.name: Optional reverse-proxy read routing plan
execution_stages.description: Do not execute in 19.5. Consider only after successful stage_1 soak and separate approval.
execution_stages.rollback: Restore previous Caddy config and reload Caddy.

monitoring_evidence: Response header X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing: hit for proxied reads during stage_1
Python and Go logs showing GET-only routed activity
No Go write attempts and no Go migration logs
success_criteria: Python prism.service remains active and is the rollback target
Go sidecar serves only the approved GET read surface
All representative reads return matching status and expected JSON shape
Fallback to Python works by disabling PRISM_GO_READ_ROUTING
No production DB write or migration is performed by Go
failure_criteria: Any write/file/maintenance route is routed to Go
Read responses drift from Python contract
Caddy or service routing exposes Prism without trusted LAN/VPN/tunnel/protected reverse proxy boundary
Any live execution proceeds without explicit approval

primary: Verify representative GET reads work without X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing
db_recovery: Use timestamped DB backup only if Python DB health is damaged
Do not let Go perform DB repair or migration

allowed_next_step.title: Approved Read-only Soak Execution
allowed_next_step.scope: execute stage_0 and optionally stage_1 from the 19.5 plan on the target host after explicit approval, with live evidence and rollback drill
